failure_modes.text: High-level leverage talk can create a finished-thinking illusion while causal assumptions and ownership remain thin.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require causal specificity: after identifying high-leverage work, name the exact mechanism by which it drives the outcome and assign an owner to each causal link.

failure_modes.text: Teams can spend their effort on safe low-value tasks that feel productive while avoiding the truly leveraged work.
failure_modes.source_quote: Focus on Triviality (Parkinson's Law of Triviality)
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit effort allocation against leverage: track where the team actually spends time and flag any pattern of gravitating toward safe, low-value tasks while avoiding the hard leveraged work.

failure_modes.text: Elegant high-value analysis can still solve the wrong problem if the underlying problem definition is flawed.
failure_modes.mitigation: Validate the problem definition before pursuing the high-value solution: check that the underlying problem is correctly framed so that elegant analysis does not solve the wrong question.

premortem_questions.text: What concrete end-product would prove that this so-called high-value focus is real rather than rhetorical?
premortem_questions.source_quote: define the specific end-product that would actually resolve the issue

premortem_questions.text: Which allegedly leveraged activity would still matter if we had to justify it with operational evidence tomorrow?
premortem_questions.source_quote: high-level framework thinking drifts from the concrete, operational evidence

premortem_questions.text: What low-value busyness are we preserving because it feels complete or safe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: many tasks look valuable on the surface but only a few meaningfully move the outcome

heuristics.text: Start from the end-product needed to resolve the issue and prune everything that does not clearly move it.
heuristics.source_quote: The team can distinguish leverage from busyness and define the specific end-product that would actually resolve the issue.

heuristics.text: Use strong early hypotheses to focus analysis on the few drivers that actually matter.
heuristics.source_quote: The process should be guided by strong hypotheses

heuristics.text: Ruthlessly narrow away completeness theater when time, attention, or capital are scarce.
heuristics.source_quote: ruthless pruning is more important than completeness theater

curation_notes.summary: Focused on identifying a small set of real drivers and pruning false completeness rather than on abstract value language.
donor_drops: Dropped broader donor-style elaboration in favor of three compact source-grounded intervention chunks per type.
open_questions: Relation richness stays in Wave 3; Wave 2 only preserves the intervention surface needed for preview substrate use.

allies: Wave 3 preserves the first-order ally surface where the raw source gives a clean current-vocabulary seam.
antagonists: Wave 3 preserves the strongest current-vocabulary conflicts and leaves weaker cross-model seams omitted on purpose.
structured_tensions: Wave 4 remains observational only; this batch does not widen higher-order source coverage.
